steps.title: Recognize the graph as the mass constraint equation
steps.content:
The line represents all valid combinations of M-type and K-type stars. Let $m$ = mass of each M-type star and $k$ = mass of each K-type star. The total mass constraint is:
$$mx + ky = 132,778$$
Reading from the graph: x-intercept at $(160, 0)$ and y-intercept at $(0, 140)$.

steps.title: Find the mass per M-type star using the x-intercept
steps.content:
At the x-intercept, all 132,778 quettagrams comes from M-type stars only (since $y = 0$):
$$m(160) + k(0) = 132,778$$
$$160m = 132,778$$
$$m = \frac{132,778}{160} = 829.8625$$
The closest value is 828. That matches choice A — A is correct.
